name: FC Santa Coloma
content:
FC Santa Coloma is a professional football club based in Santa Coloma, Andorra la Vella, and was founded in 1986. It is one of the founding members of the Andorran top division, the Primera Divisió, and has consistently competed in the country’s highest football level since its creation. The club represents the village of Santa Coloma and is one of the most recognizable teams in Andorran football.

Over the years, FC Santa Coloma has become the most successful club in Andorra. It has won multiple league titles and domestic cups, making it a dominant force in national competitions. The team has also regularly participated in UEFA competitions such as the Champions League and Europa League qualifiers, representing Andorra on the European stage.

The club plays its home matches in federation stadiums in Andorra due to the limited infrastructure in the country. In recent years, it has also developed international collaborations and changed ownership, which has helped maintain its professional structure and competitiveness. Despite its small size, FC Santa Coloma remains one of the key clubs in Andorran football history.

name: Inter Club d'Escaldes
content:
Inter Club d’Escaldes is a professional football club based in Escaldes-Engordany, Andorra. The club was founded in 1991 and became one of the founding members of the Andorran Primera Divisió in 1995. After a brief relegation in 2015, the team returned to the top division in 2017 and has since re-established itself as one of the strongest clubs in the country.

The club’s major breakthrough came in the 2019–20 season when Inter won its first league title. This success marked a turning point in its history, as the team went on to become a dominant force in Andorran football, winning multiple league titles in the following seasons. Inter has also regularly competed in domestic cup competitions such as the Copa Constitució, where it has achieved several victories.

On the international stage, Inter Club d’Escaldes has represented Andorra in UEFA competitions, including the UEFA Champions League and UEFA Europa Conference League qualifiers. Although results against stronger European clubs have been challenging, participation has helped the club gain experience and visibility. Today, Inter is considered one of the most successful and competitive teams in Andorra’s modern football era.

name: CE Principat
content:
CE Principat is a football club from Andorra la Vella, founded in 1989. It was one of the founding members of the Andorran top division, the Primera Divisió, when the national league system was established in 1995. The club is considered one of the historic teams in Andorran football, especially due to its dominance during the late 1990s.

During its golden era, CE Principat became one of the strongest clubs in Andorra, winning three consecutive league titles from 1997 to 1999 and achieving multiple domestic cup victories in the same period. It also took part in early UEFA competitions, becoming one of the first Andorran teams to represent the country in Europe, although it struggled against much stronger international opponents.

After its successful period, the club’s performance declined over time, and it eventually left the top division following relegation. CE Principat withdrew from competition in 2015, which marked the end of its active football operations. Despite this, the club remains an important part of Andorra’s football history due to its achievements in the 1990s.

name: UE Sant Julià
content:
UE Sant Julià is a football club based in Sant Julià de Lòria, Andorra, founded in 1982. It is one of the traditional clubs in Andorran football and has competed in the country’s top division since the establishment of the national league system in 1995. The club is commonly known as Sant Julià or “els lauredians”, representing the southern parish of Andorra.

During its history, UE Sant Julià has achieved significant domestic success. The club has won the Andorran championship twice and has been especially strong in cup competitions, securing multiple Copa Constitució titles. Its most successful period came in the mid-2000s and early 2010s, when it regularly competed for trophies and established itself as one of the strongest teams in the country.

UE Sant Julià has also taken part in UEFA competitions, representing Andorra in European qualifiers such as the Champions League and Europa League. Although results in Europe have been limited due to strong opposition, the club has occasionally made history for Andorran football, including being one of the first teams from the country to progress in European qualifying rounds.

name: FC Lusitanos
content:
FC Lusitanos was a football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra, founded in 1999 by Portuguese immigrants. The club had a strong Portuguese identity, reflected in its name, crest, and squad composition, as many players and staff came from Portugal or Portuguese communities in Andorra. It competed in the Andorran football league system, starting in the Segona Divisió before quickly reaching the top tier, the Primera Divisió.

The club experienced its most successful period in the early 2010s. FC Lusitanos won the Primera Divisió twice, in the 2011–12 and 2012–13 seasons, establishing itself as one of the strongest teams in Andorra at the time. It also won domestic trophies such as the Copa Constitució and the Supercopa Andorrana, and regularly qualified for UEFA competitions, including Champions League and Europa League qualifying rounds.

In European competitions, however, the club struggled against stronger opposition and often exited in the early qualifying rounds. After a decline in later years, FC Lusitanos faced financial and sporting difficulties, and the club was ultimately dissolved in 2020. Despite its disappearance, it remains an important part of Andorran football history due to its league titles and contribution to the development of the sport in the country.

name: FC Encamp
content:
FC Encamp is a football club based in Encamp, Andorra, founded in 1950. It is one of the oldest football clubs in the country and is often referred to as the “dean” of Andorran football due to its long history. The club has competed in both the Primera Divisió and Segona Divisió since the Andorran league system was officially established in 1995.

Throughout its history, FC Encamp has achieved several notable successes in domestic competitions. The club has won the Andorran top division twice, with titles in the mid-1990s and early 2000s, and has also earned promotion multiple times after relegations. It has participated in European competitions such as the UEFA Cup and UEFA Intertoto Cup, representing Andorra on the international stage.

FC Encamp is also known for being the only club in Andorra with its own dedicated stadium facilities, located in Encamp. The team has experienced fluctuating performance over the years, moving between divisions, but remains an important and historic part of Andorran football due to its longevity and contributions to the development of the sport in the country.

name: FC Rànger's
content:
FC Rànger’s is a football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra, founded in 1981. The club began competing in Andorran football in 1999 and quickly rose through the system, reaching the top division (Primera Divisió) in the early 2000s. Despite its name, the club has no connection to the Scottish side Rangers FC.

The club’s most successful period came in the mid-2000s, when FC Rànger’s became one of the strongest teams in Andorra. It won back-to-back league titles in the 2005–06 and 2006–07 seasons and also claimed the Supercopa Andorrana in 2006. During this time, the club established itself as a regular contender for domestic trophies.

On the European stage, FC Rànger’s represented Andorra in several UEFA competitions, including the UEFA Intertoto Cup, UEFA Cup, and UEFA Champions League qualifiers. Although the team was eliminated in early rounds, it made history as the first Andorran club to compete in Champions League qualifying. After its peak, the club experienced decline and spent multiple seasons in lower divisions, though it has continued to compete in Andorran football.

name: UE Santa Coloma
content:
UE Santa Coloma is a football club based in Santa Coloma, Andorra la Vella, founded in 1986. The club competes in the Andorran Primera Divisió, the country’s top football division, and plays its home matches at the Estadi Comunal d’Aixovall. It is often considered one of the modern competitive clubs in Andorran football, sharing a local rivalry with FC Santa Coloma.

The club’s rise began in the late 2000s, when it earned promotion to the top division after winning the Segona Divisió in the 2007–08 season. Since then, UE Santa Coloma has grown into a strong domestic side, regularly competing for European qualification spots and becoming a consistent presence in the Primera Divisió.

UE Santa Coloma has also achieved notable success in domestic cup competitions, winning multiple Copa Constitució titles and adding a league championship in the 2023–24 season. This success marked a historic breakthrough for the club, alongside appearances in UEFA competitions such as the Champions League and Conference League qualifiers, where it represents Andorra internationally.

name: Atlètic Club d'Escaldes
content:
Atlètic Club d’Escaldes is a football club based in Escaldes-Engordany, Andorra, founded in 2002. The club started its journey in the Andorran football system in the Segona Divisió and quickly established itself as a competitive side, earning promotion to the Primera Divisió after the 2003–04 season. After spending several seasons in the top flight, the club was relegated in 2007 but later returned to the top division in 2019 after winning the second tier.

Since its return to the Primera Divisió, Atlètic Club d’Escaldes has become one of the strongest modern teams in Andorran football. The club achieved major success by winning its first Copa Constitució in 2022 and went on to win its first league title in the 2022–23 season, marking a historic milestone in its development. It also added further domestic honours, including the Supercopa Andorrana.

On the international stage, Atlètic has represented Andorra in UEFA competitions such as the Champions League and UEFA Europa Conference League qualifiers. While facing strong European opposition has been challenging, the club has gained valuable experience and exposure through these matches. Today, Atlètic Club d’Escaldes is considered one of the leading clubs in the country’s recent football era.

name: Constel·lació Esportiva
content:
Constel·lació Esportiva was a football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra. It was founded in 1998 and competed in the Andorran top division, the Primera Divisió, during its short existence. Despite being a newly created club, it quickly became one of the strongest teams in the country at the time.

The club’s most remarkable season was 1999–2000, when it dominated domestic competitions. Constel·lació Esportiva won both the league title and the Copa Constitució, finishing the league season undefeated with an extremely strong goal difference. This dominance also earned them qualification for European competition.

However, after their successful season, the club became involved in controversy with the Andorran Football Federation, including accusations of financial irregularities and disputes over competition rules. As a result, the federation expelled the club from the league, and Constel·lació Esportiva was dissolved shortly afterward, making its existence very short but historically significant in Andorran football.

name: UE Engordany
content:
UE Engordany is a football club based in Escaldes-Engordany, Andorra. It was founded in 1980 and later became officially affiliated with the Andorran Football Federation in 2001, when it entered the national league system. The club plays its home matches in federation-managed stadiums such as the Estadi Comunal d’Aixovall.

Throughout its history, UE Engordany has moved between divisions in Andorran football, experiencing both promotions and relegations. It first reached the Primera Divisió in 2003 but was relegated after one season, later returning again in 2007 after winning promotion playoffs. In more recent years, the club established itself as a stable top-flight side and even achieved a high league finish, earning opportunities to compete for European qualification.

One of the club’s biggest achievements came in 2019, when UE Engordany won the Copa Constitució, marking its first major domestic trophy. This success also led to participation in UEFA competitions such as the Europa League qualifiers, giving the club international experience despite early exits against stronger European opponents. Today, UE Engordany is considered a competitive Andorran club with a history of steady development and occasional breakthrough success.

name: FC Andorra
content:
FC Andorra is a professional football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra, founded in 1942. Despite being located in Andorra, the club has historically competed in the Spanish football league system since 1948, as it is affiliated with the Catalan Football Federation. This makes it a unique case in European football, as it plays outside its home country’s league structure.

The club is known by the nickname “Els Tricolors” due to its traditional colors—blue, yellow, and red—which reflect the Andorran flag. FC Andorra has experienced significant growth in recent years, especially after being acquired by a group led by former Spanish international footballer Gerard Piqué. Under this ownership, the club achieved promotion through the Spanish league system and established itself at a professional level.

FC Andorra plays its home matches in Encamp and continues to compete within the Spanish football pyramid. Although it does not participate in Andorran domestic competitions, the club remains one of the most historically important football institutions in the country and a key representative of Andorran football internationally.

name: UE Extremenya
content:
UE Extremenya is a football club based in La Massana, Andorra, founded in 1998. The team currently competes in the Segona Divisió, the second tier of Andorran football. Over its history, the club has been known for frequent name changes, originally being founded as CE Cerni before becoming FC Cerni and finally adopting its current name in 2003.

The club has had a turbulent sporting history, moving between divisions multiple times. It achieved promotion to the Primera Divisió on several occasions but struggled to remain there, never managing to stay in the top flight for more than one season. Its campaigns in the top division were often difficult, including a particularly poor 2005–06 season when the team earned just one point in 20 matches.

Despite limited success at the highest level, UE Extremenya has remained a persistent competitor in Andorran football. The club has regularly participated in promotion playoffs from the second division but often fell short of returning to the top tier. Even so, it continues to be part of the national football structure, representing La Massana and maintaining its presence in domestic competitions.

name: FC Pas de la Casa
content:
FC Pas de la Casa is a football club from Andorra, based in the parish of Encamp, founded in 2012. The club currently competes in the Primera Divisió, the top tier of Andorran football. Despite being relatively young, it has quickly become part of the national league system and represents the town of Pas de la Casa.

The club’s early years were unstable, initially focusing on futsal before attempting to enter the traditional football league structure. Its first participation in the Andorran league system in 2015 was unsuccessful, as the team withdrew during the season and its results were annulled. After a long break, the club returned in the 2022–23 season and achieved immediate success by winning the Segona Divisió title with an unbeaten campaign, earning promotion to the top division.

Following promotion, FC Pas de la Casa continued to develop, reaching the final of the Copa Constitució shortly after returning to top-level football, although it finished as runner-up. The club has also built a local rivalry with FC Encamp. While still a relatively new team, it has quickly established itself as a competitive side in Andorran football.
stadium: Centre d'Entrenament de la Massana, Cap. 500
